title: Handling Mail, Deliveries, and the Home in the First Week
summary: A few small, practical home-related steps in the first week prevent bigger headaches later, without requiring much effort now.
category: After a Loss I: The First Weeks
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Handling Mail, Deliveries, and the Home in the First Week | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A few small, practical home-related steps in the first week prevent bigger headaches later, without requiring much effort now.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Forwarding or holding mail is a simple, high-value first step.
Perishables and pets need immediate attention; most everything else doesn't.
A house left empty for a stretch may need a check-in plan.

paragraphs: Setting up mail holding or forwarding through the postal service is a small task that prevents both clutter and a slow trickle of things needing attention later. Similarly, checking on perishable food, plants, and any pets is worth handling promptly.
If the home will sit empty for a while — during travel for a funeral, for instance — arranging for a neighbor or family member to check in occasionally is a reasonable, low-effort safeguard.

nextSteps: Set up mail holding or forwarding.
Handle perishables, plants, and pets promptly.
Arrange a check-in plan if the home will be empty for a stretch.
disclaimer: These are small, practical steps — none require immediate expertise or urgency beyond the basics.
